summary: The Docker-based reproduction clones the pinned wolfssl-py revision, builds the wrapper in-container, and shows that the default client APIs complete a TLS handshake with a self-signed server and exchange attacker-controlled data, while a CERT_REQUIRED control client rejects the same server.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should allow an attacker-controlled TLS server with a self-signed certificate to complete the default client handshake and return the proof payload, while a CERT_REQUIRED control client should reject the same server.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit 3e4ec8484403ea16db79cf4ebde10b5b36d944b3, checked out the wolfSSL submodule at v5.8.2-stable, built wolfssl-py in the container, and printed EVOHUNT_DEFAULT_CLIENT_ACCEPTED_SELF_SIGNED, EVOHUNT_MITM_PROOF, and EVOHUNT_CONTROL_CERT_REQUIRED_REJECTED_SELF_SIGNED before exiting successfully.
verification_method: verify.sh copies the pinned checkout into a temporary container workspace, applies an ephemeral arm64-only build-flag fix if needed so the sources compile natively, builds the wrapper in place, launches exploit.py against a generated self-signed TLS server, records the run log, and requires both the insecure-acceptance proof strings and the rejecting CERT_REQUIRED control path.
evidence_produced_by_run: The exploit run printed EVOHUNT_DEFAULT_CLIENT_ACCEPTED_SELF_SIGNED after the default client completed a TLS handshake with the attacker-controlled self-signed server.
The exploit run printed EVOHUNT_MITM_PROOF after the attacker-controlled TLS server returned its chosen payload over the accepted connection.
The exploit run printed EVOHUNT_CONTROL_CERT_REQUIRED_REJECTED_SELF_SIGNED, showing that the same server is rejected when CERT_REQUIRED is explicitly enabled.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, initializes the wolfSSL submodule to v5.8.2-stable, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
